text: Last class you asked for help when you felt sick.
Today you call for help in an emergency.
This is an emergency. I need help!
Emergency services. How can I help you?
This is an emergency. There is an accident on the street.
Stay calm. What is your address?
I am at Garden View Hotel, on Park Street. A person is hurt. Please send an ambulance!
Okay. The ambulance and the police are on the way. Do not hang up.
Thank you. I will stay on the phone.

Emergency services, how can I help you? Please stay calm and speak slowly. First, tell me what happened. Is it a fire, an accident, or a medical problem? Next, tell me where you are. Give me the name of the street and the number of the building. Then, tell me if someone is hurt. If a person is hurt, do not move them. Help is on the way. We will send the police, an ambulance, or the fire department. Please stay on the phone with me. Do not hang up. Tell me your name and a phone number. Everything will be okay. Help is coming now.

Hi Marlene, this is the hotel reception, Mister Costa. I am calling to help you. If you have an emergency in your room, please call number nine on the phone. For a fire, leave the room and use the stairs, not the elevator. For a medical problem, we can call an ambulance for you. The number for the police is right next to the phone. There is also a small first-aid box in the bathroom. If you feel in danger, call me any time, day or night. Please save my number. Stay safe, and have a good evening.

First, there is an emergency and you feel in danger. Then you pick up the phone and call for help. After that, you tell the operator what happened. Next, you give your address and say if someone is hurt. Finally, you stay calm and wait for the police or the ambulance.
There is an accident on the street.
This is an emergency. I need help!
